mission.title: Install and Configure Konveyor on Kubernetes
mission.description: Konveyor is an open-source project that provides tools for developers and platform teams to analyze and transform legacy applications for Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. Installing the Konveyor Operator allows you to manage the deployment and lifecycle of Konveyor on your Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Add the Konveyor Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Konveyor Helm repository to your local Helm configuration to access the Konveyor charts.
```bash
helm repo add konveyor https://charts.konveyor.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the Konveyor Operator
steps.description:
Install the Konveyor Operator using Helm. This command will create a new namespace called `konveyor` and deploy the operator within it.
```bash
helm install konveyor konveyor/konveyor --namespace konveyor --create-namespace --version v99.0.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Konveyor Operator pod is running successfully in the `konveyor` namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace konveyor
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Konveyor Operator to ensure it has adequate resources. Edit the Helm release to set resource limits.
```bash
helm upgrade konveyor konveyor/konveyor --namespace konveyor --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Set up Ingress (optional)
steps.description:
If you want to expose the Konveyor UI, set up Ingress by creating an Ingress resource. Replace `<your-domain>` with your actual domain.
```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: konveyor-ingress
  namespace: konveyor
spec:
  rules:
  - host: <your-domain>
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: konveyor-ui
            port:
              number: 80
```
Apply the Ingress resource:
```bash
kubectl apply -f konveyor-ingress.yaml
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Konveyor Operator pod running in the `konveyor` namespace. You can access the Konveyor UI through the configured Ingress if set up.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add konveyor https://charts.konveyor.io
helm repo update
helm install konveyor konveyor/konveyor --namespace konveyor --create-namespace --version v99.0.0
helm upgrade konveyor konveyor/konveyor --namespace konveyor --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: konveyor-ingress
  namespace: konveyor
spec:
  rules:
  - host: <your-domain>
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: konveyor-ui
            port:
              number: 80

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with OLM and Ingress support.
